senses.definition: a two-player board game in which each player has 15 pieces that can move along 24 long triangles called points based on the throw of dice, the winner is the first player who manages to move all their pieces out of the board

examples.x: She played backgammon with her friends every weekend to unwind.
He won the backgammon match by getting all his checkers off the board before his opponent.
After rolling the dice, she moved her checkers closer to her home board in backgammon.
They enjoyed playing backgammon, even though the game could get competitive at times.

senses.definition: a two-player board game with 12 pieces per side, played on an 8x8 grid, aiming to capture the opponent's pieces through diagonal moves and jumps

examples.x: She taught her younger brother how to play checkers on their rainy afternoon at home.
After a few rounds of checkers, they decided to switch to chess for a more challenging game.
The checkers game was intense, with each player carefully planning their moves.
They sat down for a friendly match of checkers, both eager to see who would win.
He lost the game of checkers when his opponent managed to trap all of his pieces.

senses.definition: a board game for 2-6 players in which each player has 10 marbles and attempts to move them from one hole to another on a star-shaped surface
an indoor game played on a table with holes and pins on it, in which each player attempts to hit the ball in a way that they move past the pins and go into the holes each indicating a specific score
a children's board game in which players aim to reach a target point at the top of the board and the pictures of ladders help them move forward while those of the chutes set them back

examples.x: After dinner, we played Chutes and Ladders to pass the time.
The kids were excited when they reached the top of the ladder in Chutes and Ladders.
We spent hours playing Chutes and Ladders at the family reunion.
She was almost at the finish line in Chutes and Ladders but then fell down a chute.

senses.definition: a simple board game for two to four players, in which players advance counters according to dice rolls
a type of board game in which players use game currency to buy buildings or streets represented on the board
a type of board game in which one tries to make words on a board using small blocks with letters on them
a children's board game, with pictures of snakes and ladders, in which players move their pieces according to a throw of dice

examples.x: We played snakes and ladders all afternoon and had so much fun.
My little brother loves playing snakes and ladders because it's easy to understand.
The kids took turns rolling the die in their game of snakes and ladders.
We decided to play snakes and ladders during the power outage to pass the time.

senses.definition: a game played on a flat surface, for one or two pairs of players who aim to get small discs into a cup, to do so, they must apply pressure to these small disks by a larger disk in a way that pops them up to the cup

examples.x: We spent the afternoon playing tiddlywinks, and I finally won after several close attempts.
I had never played tiddlywinks before, but it was surprisingly enjoyable.
After a few rounds of tiddlywinks, everyone started to get really competitive.
We played a fun game of tiddlywinks at the family gathering last night.

senses.definition: a two-player abstract strategy board game designed to be strategically deep and challenging, featuring animal-themed pieces and a unique movement system

examples.x: The camel in Arimaa is one of the strongest pieces, able to move long distances.
After learning the rules of chess, I decided to try Arimaa for a change of pace.
Some players find Arimaa to be more challenging than chess because it requires constant adaptation.
My friend and I spent hours playing Arimaa, trying to outsmart each other with clever moves.
Arimaa requires strategic thinking, as each piece has unique movement rules.

senses.definition: a type of tabletop game that involves players embarking on a fictional journey or quest, typically exploring a themed setting, overcoming challenges, and making decisions that impact the outcome of the game

examples.x: My friends and I enjoy playing an adventure board game every weekend.
We spent the whole evening trying to finish the adventure board game, but we kept getting stuck on the same challenge.
The adventure board game was so engaging that we lost track of time and played for hours.
She loves playing adventure board games because they allow her to use her imagination.
They decided to buy a new adventure board game for their game night.

senses.definition: a two-player abstract strategy board game where the players take turns placing stones on a grid to form a line of five stones horizontally, vertically, or diagonally to win

examples.x: He won the game of gomoku by lining up five pieces diagonally.
They played gomoku for hours, trying to outsmart each other with every move.
She placed her stone carefully, hoping to create a line of five in gomoku.
The game of gomoku ended quickly because neither player could predict the other's next move.

senses.definition: a type of tabletop game that focuses on pure strategy and lacks theme or narrative elements, typically played on a grid or board with simple rules and without chance or luck-based elements
a historical two-player board game of French origin, similar to backgammon, where players move their pieces based on dice rolls and try to bear off all their pieces before the opponent

examples.x: He learned to play trictrac from his grandfather, who had a vintage board set.
They spent the afternoon playing trictrac, trying to outsmart each other with every move.
I had never heard of trictrac before, but it seemed interesting when my friend explained the rules.

senses.definition: an ancient Indian board game played with dice and pieces, similar to Pachisi, where players race to move their pieces around the board and capture opponents' pieces to win

examples.x: The players took turns rolling the dice in chaupar, hoping to land on the right spaces.
He enjoyed playing chaupar with his family during the festival, as it was a way to bond and have fun.
She moved her pieces carefully, trying to block her opponents while advancing in chaupar.

senses.definition: an ancient Indian cross and circle board game played with dice and pieces, where players race to move their pieces around the board and capture opponents' pieces to reach the center and win

examples.x: We spent the afternoon playing pachisi, a game that has been enjoyed in India for centuries.

senses.definition: an ancient two-player strategy board game that has been played in various forms for thousands of years
a two-player guessing game that is played on a board with two grids. Each player has their own grid, and each grid is divided into rows and columns, with each cell identified by a letter and a number
a Canadian tabletop game where players use their fingers to flick small discs into scoring areas on a round wooden board with pockets or holes

examples.x: We couldn't stop playing pitchnut; it became the highlight of our weekend.
I love how pitchnut requires both accuracy and strategy to win.
We spent the afternoon playing pitchnut, trying to land our discs in the corner pockets.

senses.definition: a classic Canadian tabletop dexterity game played on a round wooden board with a hole in the center, where players use their fingers to flick discs, aiming to score points by landing them in higher-scoring areas and knocking opponents' discs out of play

examples.x: He used to be terrible at crokinole, but now he’s unbeatable after practicing his flicking technique for months.
Every Sunday, my family gathers for a friendly crokinole tournament, and we compete for bragging rights.
We set up a crokinole board at the party, and everyone had so much fun trying to get their discs into the center.
During the weekend, we had a game night and played crokinole, which was much more intense than I expected!

senses.definition: a strategic board game played on a checkered board where players flick disks, trying to capture the opponent's disks and achieve specific objectives to win
a strategic two-player board game where players move pieces with different abilities across a board with the objective of capturing the opponent's king

examples.x: Chess requires strategic thinking and planning several moves ahead to outmaneuver your opponent.
The knight is one of the most versatile pieces in chess, capable of jumping over other pieces in its path.
Playing chess regularly can improve cognitive skills such as problem-solving and decision-making.
The world championship in chess is a highly prestigious event, attracting the best players from around the globe.
They used an online app to play chess together.

senses.definition: an abstract strategy board game where players take turns placing colorful pieces on the board, attempting to cover as much area as possible while blocking their opponents from doing the same

a tile-based game using rectangular pieces with varying numbers of dots on each end, and players take turns matching the dots on one end of their dominoes to those on the board to form a line, aiming to score points or be the first to play all their tiles

examples.x: We played dominoes all afternoon and had a great time with friends.
The kids were excited to play dominoes after school.
She won the game of dominoes by placing her last tile with a perfect match.
After a long day, we gathered around the table for a few rounds of dominoes.

senses.definition: an originally Chinese game that is often played by 4 people and is consisted of 144 tiles and small wooden pieces

examples.x: My family gathers every weekend to play mahjong, and it's always a lot of fun.
After learning the rules, she quickly became skilled at mahjong and won most of the games.

senses.definition: a tile-based game where players aim to form sets and runs of numbered tiles to score points and win by using all the tiles in their hand first
a tile-based board game where players aim to create sets and runs of numbered tiles, combining elements of rummy and mahjong, to be the first to use all their tiles and win

a tile-based board game that is a variation of dominoes, where players match and place curved tiles with different numbers to create a connected network of curves and score points based on the numbers shown on the curves

a tile-based board game similar to dominoes but with triangular-shaped tiles, where players match and place tiles with numbers on their edges to form a triangular pattern and score points based on the numbers shown on the tiles
a dominoes game played with a set of double-nine dominoes, where players try to play all their tiles by matching them to the central spinner or to other open ends of the layout
a tile-matching game where players draw and play tiles to match the numbers on the ends of a line of dominoes, aiming to be the first to play all their tiles or score the fewest points
a dominoes game using double-twelve dominoes, where players aim to match and play their tiles to get rid of them and be the first to empty their hand
a tile-based board game where players match and place tiles with different shapes and colors to create lines and score points
a tile-based game where players match and place curved tiles with different numbers to create a connected network of curves and score points

examples.x: I bought a new game called quad-ominos and we played it all night.
We had so much fun trying to match the numbers in quad-ominos.
After a few rounds, I started getting better at quad-ominos and managed to win.
He couldn’t make a move in quad-ominos, so he had to draw a tile.

senses.definition: an ancient two-player strategy board game that originated in China over 2,500 years ago
a deduction board game where one player creates a secret code, and the other player attempts to guess the code by making guesses and receiving feedback on the correctness of their guesses
a strategy board game where two players command armies of different ranks and attempt to capture the opponent's flag while protecting their own
an ancient Egyptian board game with religious significance, where players move pieces along a track to reach the end while avoiding obstacles, making it one of the oldest known board games in history
a strategic board game for two players who take turns placing pieces on the board, with the objective of flipping the opponent's pieces to their own color to win

an abstract strategy board game played on a hexagonal grid, where two players aim to connect their opposite sides of the board with an unbroken chain of their pieces, blocking their opponent from doing the same

a classic murder mystery board game where players assume the roles of detectives, attempting to solve a murder by gathering clues, making deductions, and determining the culprit, weapon, and location of the crime

examples.x: Cluedo is a fun way to spend an evening with friends, as it challenges you to think logically and solve a mystery.
After a few rounds of Cluedo, we started to figure out who had the best strategy for making accurate suggestions.
She loves playing Cluedo, but she gets frustrated when someone else solves the mystery before she does.
The game of Cluedo dragged on for hours, with everyone making guesses but never quite hitting the right combination.
He was really close to winning Cluedo, but he made one wrong guess and lost the game at the last minute.

senses.definition: a two-team word association game where players give one-word clues to help their teammates guess which words on a grid belong to their team, while avoiding the words that belong to the opposing team or the "assassin" word

examples.x: The spymaster gave a clever clue in Codenames, and we quickly guessed three words.
We played Codenames last night, and it was so fun trying to connect words with just one clue.
Our team is doing well in Codenames because our spymaster is great at giving hints.
I love playing Codenames with my friends; it's a fun challenge to figure out the connection between words.
During the game of Codenames, we struggled to avoid the assassin word, but we managed to win.

senses.definition: a tabletop role-playing game where players create characters, embark on quests, and engage in imaginative storytelling together, led by a game master who adjudicates the rules and controls the non-player characters and world

examples.x: We spent the weekend playing Dungeons & Dragons, and I finally leveled up my character.
He’s the Dungeon Master for our Dungeons & Dragons group, and he’s great at creating exciting quests.
I love playing Dungeons & Dragons because it challenges my creativity and problem-solving skills.

senses.definition: a classic German board game for 2-4 players that involves rolling dice and moving pieces around the board to be the first player to move all their pieces into their respective "home" spaces
a strategic board game played on a 15x15 grid, where players aim to form a row of five stones in a row while blocking their opponent from doing the same

a board game that is often used as a tool for communicating with spirits or other supernatural entities, where players rest their hands on a planchette that moves to spell out messages on the board in response to questions asked by the players

examples.x: They decided to try the Ouija after hearing so many stories about its mysterious powers.
Many people believe that using the Ouija can open doors to the spirit world.
She was skeptical about the Ouija but agreed to give it a try with her friends.
After the Ouija session, they all felt a strange presence in the room that lasted for hours.

senses.definition: a tabletop game that originated in India, played on a square board with pockets at the corners, where players use a striker and small disks to shoot and pocket their own colored disks, with the objective of sinking all their pieces before their opponent does

examples.x: After practicing for hours, he became very good at carrom and could easily win against anyone.
The game of carrom has always been a favorite pastime in our family, especially during the holidays.
